steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is a transition between two sentences about Harris's poem. The first says the poem “suggests the fluid interplay of poetry and painting.” The second says the poem “highlights fundamental tensions between these modes of representation.” These are two simultaneous, contrasting truths about the same poem — the blank must signal contrast or coexistence, not restatement, causation, or example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In other words,’: signals restatement. The second sentence says something different from (not a paraphrase of) the first. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Therefore,’: signals a conclusion caused by the first sentence. Interplay doesn't logically produce fundamental tensions. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘At the same time,’: signals that both things are simultaneously true about the poem. That matches two coexisting (and contrasting) observations. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Specifically,’: signals that the second sentence is a specific example of the first. Fundamental tensions are not a specific example of fluid interplay — they are the opposite. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
